verses.text: Aaron's sons; Nadab and Abihu died, so Eleazar and Ithamar minister. Aaron's sons. The priestly line continues.
Aaron's sons; Nadab and Abihu died, so Eleazar and Ithamar minister. Aaron's sons. The priestly line continues.
Aaron's sons; Nadab and Abihu died, so Eleazar and Ithamar minister. Aaron's sons. The priestly line continues.
Aaron's sons; Nadab and Abihu died, so Eleazar and Ithamar minister. Aaron's sons. The priestly line continues.
The Levites are given to Aaron to do the service of the tabernacle. Given. They assist the priests.
The Levites are given to Aaron to do the service of the tabernacle. Given. They assist the priests.
The Levites are given to Aaron to do the service of the tabernacle. Given. They assist the priests.
They keep the instruments and the charge; only Aaron's sons are priests. The charge. Levites serve, priests alone offer.
They keep the instruments and the charge; only Aaron's sons are priests. The charge. Levites serve, priests alone offer.
They keep the instruments and the charge; only Aaron's sons are priests. The charge. Levites serve, priests alone offer.
The Levites are taken instead of the firstborn, who are God's. Instead. God claims the Levites for the firstborn.
The Levites are taken instead of the firstborn, who are God's. Instead. God claims the Levites for the firstborn.
The Levites are taken instead of the firstborn, who are God's. Instead. God claims the Levites for the firstborn.
The Levites are numbered from a month old. Numbered. Even the young are counted to God.
The Levites are numbered from a month old. Numbered. Even the young are counted to God.
The Levites are numbered from a month old. Numbered. Even the young are counted to God.
They are reckoned by Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The families. Three branches serve.
They are reckoned by Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The families. Three branches serve.
They are reckoned by Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The families. Three branches serve.
They are reckoned by Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. The families. Three branches serve.
Gershon's families camp behind the tabernacle, westward. Gershon. Their place is set.
Gershon's families camp behind the tabernacle, westward. Gershon. Their place is set.
Gershon's families camp behind the tabernacle, westward. Gershon. Their place is set.
Their charge is the curtains, hangings, and cords. The curtains. They bear the coverings.
Their charge is the curtains, hangings, and cords. The curtains. They bear the coverings.
Their charge is the curtains, hangings, and cords. The curtains. They bear the coverings.
Kohath's families camp on the south side. Kohath. Their place is set.
Kohath's families camp on the south side. Kohath. Their place is set.
Kohath's families camp on the south side. Kohath. Their place is set.
Their charge is the ark, table, candlestick, and vessels. The holiest. They bear the sacred things.
Their charge is the ark, table, candlestick, and vessels. The holiest. They bear the sacred things.
Their charge is the ark, table, candlestick, and vessels. The holiest. They bear the sacred things.
Merari's charge is the boards, bars, and pillars. Merari. They bear the frame.
Merari's charge is the boards, bars, and pillars. Merari. They bear the frame.

Moses and Aaron camp before the tabernacle eastward. The front. The leaders guard the entrance.
Moses and Aaron camp before the tabernacle eastward. The front. The leaders guard the entrance.
The firstborn of Israel are numbered. Firstborn. God counts those who are His.
The firstborn of Israel are numbered. Firstborn. God counts those who are His.

The Levites redeem the firstborn of Israel. Redeemed. God's claim is met through them.

The surplus firstborn are redeemed with money given to Aaron. Ransom. The remainder is bought back.
